safetyChecks.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
safetyChecks.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
safetyChecks.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

requiredExam.label: Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation

source.source_section: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue. Hyperpigmentation supports primary adrenal insufficiency or ACTH excess and changes ACTH, antibody, renin/aldosterone, or steroid-replacement interpretation.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa, palmar creases, scars, and sun-protected skin for diffuse or patchy hyperpigmentation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When primary adrenal insufficiency, Addison disease, ACTH excess, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Hyperpigmentation supports primary adrenal insufficiency or ACTH excess and changes ACTH, antibody, renin/aldosterone, or steroid-replacement interpretation.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

source.source_section: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dry mucous membranes change adrenal-crisis severity assessment, fluid urgency, and stress-dose steroid planning.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting, vomiting, or poor intake is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Dry mucous membranes change adrenal-crisis severity assessment, fluid urgency, and stress-dose steroid planning.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Test chair-rise strength

source.source_section: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment. Clinically important weakness changes crisis severity, fall risk, and monitored-care threshold.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
requiredExam.technique: Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting illness, or functional weakness is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Clinically important weakness changes crisis severity, fall risk, and monitored-care threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Inspect terminal hair distribution

source.source_section: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Terminal hair pattern changes androgen-excess severity and testing priority.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When androgen excess, virilization, or nonclassic CAH is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Terminal hair pattern changes androgen-excess severity and testing priority.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Stage pubertal development

source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate pubertal development, delayed/precocious puberty pattern, or hypogonadal phenotype. Pubertal stage changes interpretation of adrenal androgen excess and gonadal-axis testing.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets pubertal development, delayed/precocious puberty pattern, or hypogonadal phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
requiredExam.technique: Assess Tanner stage or secondary sex characteristics only when clinically appropriate and with consent/chaperone practices.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When pubertal timing, growth, fertility, or classic CAH phenotype is relevant
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: pubertal development, delayed/precocious puberty pattern, or hypogonadal phenotype

requiredExam.limitations: Stage pubertal development is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: 17-OHP thresholds vary by age, gestational age, assay, cycle phase, and timing; morning follicular sampling improves adult NCCAH screening.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
